description_long: This chaise features a modern, geometric design with clean, angular lines and a solid, monolithic appearance. The seat is broad and rectangular, with a slightly sloped surface and a series of diagonal ridges or grooves on one side, adding a subtle decorative element. The backrest is tall and straight, extending the full width of the seat, and is visually integrated into the overall blocky form. The entire piece is finished in a deep, nearly black color, giving it a sleek and contemporary look suitable for urban environments. There are no visible armrests or legs, as the base appears to be a continuous solid block. The surface finish is matte, with minimal reflectivity and a hard, smooth texture.
description: A modern, blocky chaise with a tall backrest, diagonal seat grooves, and a matte black finish. The design is sleek, angular, and visually solid, with no visible armrests or legs.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full width of the chaise, highlighting the tall backrest and the diagonal grooves on the right side of the seat.
description_view_1: Right side view reveals the depth of the seat and the thickness of the backrest, as well as the angular, monolithic profile.
description_view_2: Rear view displays a plain, solid back with no visible features or decorative elements.
description_view_3: Left side view mirrors the right, showing the seat's depth and the chaise's overall blocky construction.
attribute_backrest_design: The backrest is tall, straight, and extends the full width of the chaise, with a solid, unsegmented surface and no visible decorative elements or curvature.
attribute_armrest_presence: No armrests are present on either side of the chaise.
attribute_leg_style: No visible legs; the base is a continuous, solid block that supports the entire structure.
attribute_upholstery_pattern: The upholstery is solid in color with a matte finish, featuring diagonal grooves on one side of the seat for subtle visual interest.
attribute_cushion_arrangement: There are no separate or loose cushions; the seat and backrest are integrated into the main structure, with the seat surface featuring a single set of diagonal grooves.
